udfs.name: addnoise
udfs.description: Adds Gaussian noise to an integer value and returns the result as a float.

udfs.name: clean
udfs.description: Parses a JSON-encoded list of strings, converts them to lowercase, filters out words shorter than three characters, sorts words internally within each string, and returns the alphabetically sorted overall list as a JSON string.

udfs.name: cleandate
udfs.description: Reads a date string and converts it to a standardized format, handling partial dates by defaulting the day to '01' and swapping hyphens or slashes appropriately.

udfs.name: converttoeuro
udfs.description: Converts a given currency amount to Euros based on a predefined dictionary of exchange rates.

udfs.name: extractclass
udfs.description: Extracts the class portion from a formatted project string by splitting it at the '::' delimiter and returning the second element.

udfs.name: extractcode
udfs.description: Extracts the project ID from a structured string containing the funder's ID, funding class, and project ID separated by '::'.

udfs.name: extractday
udfs.description: Extracts the day as an integer from a given date string by parsing the substring after the last hyphen.

udfs.name: extractfunder
udfs.description: Extracts the funder portion from a project string formatted as 'funder::class::projectid' by returning the substring before the first double colon.

udfs.name: extractid
udfs.description: Extracts the third element from a string separated by a double colon (::), returning None if the string is empty or lacks sufficient elements.

udfs.name: extractmonth
udfs.description: Extracts the month as an integer from a given hyphen-separated date string.

udfs.name: extractprojectid
udfs.description: Extracts the first exactly six-digit project identifier from a given text string.

udfs.name: extractyear
udfs.description: Extracts and returns the year as an integer from a provided date string.

udfs.name: filterstopwords
udfs.description: Processes an input text string and returns it after removing stopwords using a predefined multilingual list.

udfs.name: frequentterms
udfs.description: Extracts and returns the top N most frequent lowercase words from a given string as a space-separated text.

udfs.name: jaccard
udfs.description: Calculates the Jaccard similarity index between two JSON-formatted lists of tokens.

udfs.name: jpack
udfs.description: Converts a string into a JSON-formatted string containing a list of its word tokens.

udfs.name: jsoncount
udfs.description: Returns the number of elements in a JSON string if it is a list, otherwise returns 1.

udfs.name: jsonparse_q14
udfs.description: SCALAR version. Extracts ONE value from a single JSON string. Registered specifically as jsonparse_q14. Use only in SELECT for simple tasks.

udfs.name: jsort
udfs.description: Parses a JSON-formatted string representing a list, sorts its elements, and returns the result as a new JSON string, defaulting to an empty list upon failure.

udfs.name: jsortvalues
udfs.description: Parses a JSON array of strings, alphabetically sorts the space-separated tokens within each string, and returns the modified array as a JSON string.

udfs.name: keywords
udfs.description: Removes punctuation from the input text and returns the extracted alphanumeric keywords as a single space-separated string.

udfs.name: log_10
udfs.description: Calculates the base-10 logarithm of a given floating-point number, returning 0.0 in case of an exception.

udfs.name: lowerize
udfs.description: Converts the input text to lowercase.

udfs.name: removeshortterms
udfs.description: Parses a JSON-encoded list of strings and removes any space-separated tokens shorter than three characters from each string.

udfs.name: stem
udfs.description: Stems the words in the input text using the Porter2 stemming algorithm.

udfs.name: aggregate_avg
udfs.description: Calculates the average of a given series of values, ignoring nulls and non-numeric entries.Calculates average. Note: Ensure you handle duplicates correctly by grouping first if necessary.

udfs.name: aggregate_count
udfs.description: Calculates the count of non-null values in a given series.

udfs.name: aggregate_max
udfs.description: Calculates the maximum value within a series or group of values while ignoring nulls.

udfs.name: aggregate_median
udfs.description: Calculates the median of a given series of values by first coercing them to numeric types and dropping any nulls.

udfs.name: aggregate_top
udfs.description: Groups data by a specified column and Returns columns: group_column1, group_column2, top_s.

udfs.name: combinations
udfs.description: Reads a JSON-formatted list and returns a table containing all possible combinations of its elements based on a specified integer parameter.

udfs.name: combinations_q16
udfs.description: Parses a JSON-encoded list from the eighth element of an input row to generate all combinations of length N, Returns columns: pubid, pubdate, projectstart, projectend, funder, fclass, projectid, authorpair.

udfs.name: extractfromdate
udfs.description: Extracts the year, month, and day components as integers from a hyphen-separated date string and returns them alongside a provided identifier.

udfs.name: extractkeys
udfs.description: Parses a list of JSON-formatted strings and extracts the values associated with two specified keys as a set of rows.

udfs.name: file_q13
udfs.description: MANDATORY for CrossRef project data. Use: file_q13('crossref.txt', 'text'). Returns column: column1 (JSON string). Use jsonparse(TABLE(SELECT column1...)) to extract fields.You MUST pass this column to jsonparse(TABLE(SELECT column1...), 'key') to get the final values

udfs.name: file_q18
udfs.description: MANDATORY for ArXiv abstracts. Use: file_q18('arxiv.csv', 'csv'). Returns columns: column1 (id), column2 (abstract)

udfs.name: file_q7
udfs.description: MANDATORY for PubMed citations. Use: file_q7('pubmed_q7.txt', 'json'). Returns: column1 (id), column2 (citations JSON), column3 (authors JSON). MANDATORY: Use these column names.

udfs.name: jsonparse
udfs.description: TABLE version (UDTF). Extracts TWO keys from a relation of JSON strings. MANDATORY for file processing. Use ONLY in FROM clause with TABLE syntax. Returns two columns named after the keys. Example: FROM jsonparse(TABLE(SELECT column1 FROM ...), 'key1', 'key2').

udfs.name: xmlparser
udfs.description: Parses a list of XML content strings into a single document and returns a table of JSON-formatted records extracted from a specified XML element.

udfs.name: extractday_scala
udfs.description: Scala-compatible version of extractday. Extracts the day as an integer from a given date string.

udfs.name: extractmonth_java
udfs.description: Java-compatible version of extractmonth. Extracts the month as an integer from a given hyphen-separated date string.
